channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Future Leaders in the World of Work 2026: Complete Guide to the ITCILO South-South University Programme in Italy and Switzerland for Youth Leaders, Researchers, and Young Professionals
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The Future Leaders in the World of Work – South-South University 2026 is now accepting applications from ambitious university students, aspiring researchers, innovators, youth representatives, and early-career professionals who want to build careers in international development, multilateral diplomacy, sustainable development, and South-South and Triangular Cooperation (SSTC). Organized by the International Training Centre of the International […]

The post Future Leaders in the World of Work 2026: Complete Guide to the ITCILO South-South University Programme in Italy and Switzerland for Youth Leaders, Researchers, and Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Future Leaders in the World of Work – South-South University 2026 is now accepting applications from ambitious university students, aspiring researchers, innovators, youth representatives, and early-career professionals who want to build careers in international development, multilateral diplomacy, sustainable development, and South-South and Triangular Cooperation (SSTC).

Organized by the International Training Centre of the International Labour Organization (ITCILO) in cooperation with its knowledge partners and delivered within the framework of the Turin School of Development, this flagship programme equips participants with the knowledge, skills, networks, and career guidance needed to become future leaders supporting human-centred development across the Global South.

Taking place from 27 July to 7 August 2026, the programme combines face-to-face learning in Turin, Italy, a study visit to Geneva, Switzerland, practical workshops, career coaching, exposure to United Nations agencies, and opportunities to develop actionable proposals addressing the future of work.

This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the programme, including its objectives, benefits, eligibility requirements, learning structure, application process, costs, important dates, and why it represents a valuable opportunity for future global leaders.

What Is the Future Leaders in the World of Work Programme?

Future Leaders in the World of Work is a flagship international training programme under the South-South University, designed to prepare the next generation of professionals who are committed to advancing sustainable development through South-South and Triangular Cooperation.

The programme recognizes that the future of employment, international cooperation, and sustainable development is being reshaped by major global transformations, including:

  • Emerging technologies
  • Climate change
  • Demographic transitions
  • Trade and investment shifts
  • Digital transformation
  • Changing labour markets
  • New forms of multilateral cooperation

Participants explore how these global trends influence employment, development policy, and international cooperation while designing innovative responses that promote decent work and sustainable development.

The programme also supports young professionals interested in careers with:

  • United Nations agencies
  • International organizations
  • Development institutions
  • Government ministries
  • International NGOs
  • Multilateral development organizations
  • Global policy institutions

About the South-South University

The South-South University is implemented through collaboration between ITCILO and its knowledge partners within the Turin School of Development.

Its primary mission is to support ambitious young leaders who wish to contribute to:

  • Human-centred development
  • South-South Cooperation
  • Triangular Cooperation
  • International development
  • Social justice
  • Decent work
  • Sustainable economic transformation

Rather than offering only classroom-based instruction, the programme emphasizes practical engagement, collaboration, and policy-oriented problem solving.

Why This Programme Matters

The world of work is evolving rapidly.

Artificial intelligence, automation, environmental challenges, digital transformation, and shifting geopolitical dynamics are changing how societies develop and how labour markets function.

Countries in the Global South increasingly rely on South-South and Triangular Cooperation to:

  • Share successful development models
  • Exchange technical expertise
  • Promote sustainable economic growth
  • Build resilience
  • Strengthen social inclusion

The Future Leaders in the World of Work programme prepares participants to contribute meaningfully to these efforts while positioning themselves for future leadership roles in international organizations.

Programme Dates

The 2026 edition will take place from:

27 July – 7 August 2026

The programme consists of activities in both Italy and Switzerland.

Italy

  • Turin
  • 27–29 July 2026
  • 1–7 August 2026

Switzerland

  • Geneva
  • 30–31 July 2026

The programme is delivered entirely in English.

Programme Structure

The programme combines several complementary learning experiences.

1. Study Visit to Geneva

Participants travel to Geneva to gain first-hand exposure to international organizations.

Highlights include:

  • Guest lectures at ILO Headquarters
  • Learning about multilateral diplomacy
  • Understanding UN operations
  • Exploring international careers
  • Visiting selected Geneva-based organizations

Participants also meet representatives from Human Resources departments who explain career pathways within the United Nations system.

2. Thematic Workshops

Interactive workshops focus on:

  • Human-centred development
  • South-South Cooperation
  • Sustainable development
  • Future of work
  • Global labour challenges

Participants work collaboratively to develop action-oriented proposals addressing global development priorities.

3. Personalized Career Coaching

One of the programme’s distinguishing features is individualized coaching.

Participants receive support from international career coaches who help them:

  • Clarify career goals
  • Develop long-term career plans
  • Build international career strategies
  • Prepare for careers within the UN system
  • Identify opportunities in multilateral organizations

Each participant develops a realistic three-to-five-year professional roadmap.

What Participants Will Learn

Throughout the programme, participants examine major issues influencing international development.

Topics include:

  • Future of work
  • Sustainable development
  • Human-centred technology
  • Climate change
  • Trade and investment
  • Demographic transitions
  • International development
  • Multilateral diplomacy
  • South-South Cooperation
  • Triangular Cooperation
  • Social justice
  • Decent work
  • Sustainable labour markets

The curriculum encourages participants to formulate innovative solutions capable of supporting equitable and sustainable development.

Career Development Opportunities

A major strength of the programme is its emphasis on career preparation.

Participants receive guidance that includes:

  • UN career pathways
  • Career planning
  • Professional coaching
  • Networking strategies
  • Leadership development
  • International career preparation

According to ITCILO, participants benefit from career coaching typically reserved for UN fellows.

Who Can Apply?

The programme welcomes applicants from around the world who are passionate about international development and the future of work.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Youth representatives
  • University students
  • Innovators
  • Aspiring researchers
  • Young professionals

Professionals should generally be within five years of graduation at the time of application.

Researchers should be enrolled in:

  • Full-time taught degree programmes
  • Full-time research degree programmes

Academic Backgrounds

Applicants may come from numerous disciplines relevant to the International Labour Organization, including:

  • Law
  • Economics
  • Environmental Science
  • Government
  • Political Science
  • International Relations
  • Population Studies
  • Public Policy
  • Development Studies

Applicants from related disciplines are also encouraged to apply.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Commitment to South-South and Triangular Cooperation
  • Interest in sustainable development
  • Passion for the future of work
  • Strong motivation to support international development
  • Interest in the work of the International Labour Organization
  • Fluency in English

Knowledge of French or Spanish is considered an asset but is not mandatory.

Motivation Interviews

Shortlisted applicants will be invited to participate in motivation interviews.

During these interviews, candidates should explain:

  • Their academic journey
  • Professional experience
  • Motivation for joining the programme
  • Career aspirations
  • How the programme aligns with their future goals

Applicants are encouraged to clearly connect their background with the objectives of the programme.

Open House Webinars

Prospective applicants can participate in Open House Webinars designed to help them prepare competitive applications.

Topics include:

  • Application guidance
  • Motivation interview preparation
  • Understanding programme objectives
  • Exploring South-South Cooperation
  • Learning about future work trends

These webinars also provide opportunities to interact directly with programme coordinators.

Campus Experience

Participants study at the ITCILO Campus located along the banks of the Po River in Turin.

The campus offers:

  • Safe learning environment
  • International atmosphere
  • Modern facilities
  • Opportunities for networking
  • Access to the United Nations Campus

Turin has hosted the United Nations Campus since 1964 and is home to:

  • International Training Centre of the ILO
  • United Nations System Staff College
  • UN Interregional Crime and Justice Research Institute

Study Visit to Geneva

Participants spend two days in Geneva exploring:

  • International Labour Organization
  • Other UN agencies
  • International organizations
  • NGOs

Activities include:

  • Expert lectures
  • Institutional visits
  • Career sessions
  • Human Resources discussions
  • Multilateral diplomacy exposure

Optional Cultural Excursions

Participants may also register for optional day trips to:

  • Venice
  • Nice
  • Monaco

These cultural activities complement the learning experience while allowing participants to explore Europe.

Certificate

Participants who successfully complete all programme requirements receive an official ITCILO Certificate of Participation.

The certificate is issued in Digital Credentials Format, allowing recipients to:

  • Share on LinkedIn
  • Include on CVs
  • Showcase professional development
  • Demonstrate international learning experience

Programme Costs

Programme fees are:

  • Tuition: €2,345
  • Subsistence: €2,005

Total Cost: €4,350

Applicants should review programme details for payment arrangements and additional travel considerations.

Important Dates

Applicants should carefully note the following timeline:

  • Programme Dates: 27 July–7 August 2026
  • Application Deadline: 5 July 2026
  • Language: English
  • Delivery Mode: Face-to-face

The application deadline has been extended to 5 July 2026 for participants who already possess, or do not require, an entry visa for Italy.

Why You Should Apply

This programme offers much more than classroom learning.

Participants benefit from:

  • International networking
  • Exposure to the United Nations system
  • Professional coaching
  • Leadership development
  • Practical workshops
  • Career guidance
  • Policy engagement
  • Cross-cultural collaboration
  • International experience
  • Official ITCILO certification

It is particularly valuable for young professionals seeking careers in:

  • International organizations
  • Sustainable development
  • Public policy
  • Labour and employment
  • Global governance
  • International cooperation
  • Diplomacy

Click HERE to learn more about the Future Leaders in the World of Work 2026.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their application through the Official ITCILO Application Portal before the application deadline.

Applicants are encouraged to:

  1. Review all eligibility requirements.
  2. Prepare a strong application.
  3. Demonstrate motivation for international development.
  4. Highlight academic and professional achievements.
  5. Show commitment to South-South and Triangular Cooperation.
  6. Prepare for the motivation interview if shortlisted.

Download the Brochure HERE

Conclusion

The Future Leaders in the World of Work – South-South University 2026 represents an exceptional opportunity for ambitious students, researchers, innovators, and young professionals seeking to build careers in international development, sustainable development, and multilateral cooperation.

Through an immersive learning experience spanning Turin and Geneva, participants gain direct exposure to the work of the International Labour Organization, United Nations agencies, and leading experts working on the future of work. The combination of practical workshops, policy discussions, personalized career coaching, networking opportunities, and international study visits equips participants with the knowledge, confidence, and global perspective needed to contribute meaningfully to sustainable development and South-South Cooperation initiatives.

With an application deadline of 5 July 2026, interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications as early as possible, prepare thoroughly for the motivation interview, and take advantage of this unique opportunity to become part of a global community of future leaders committed to advancing decent work, social justice, and human-centred development.

Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post Future Leaders in the World of Work 2026: Complete Guide to the ITCILO South-South University Programme in Italy and Switzerland for Youth Leaders, Researchers, and Young Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Deloitte Graduate Program 2026 Applications Open: Launch Your Career with One of the World’s Leading Professional Services Firms
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Every successful career starts with the right opportunity, and Deloitte is offering aspiring professionals exactly that. Applications are now open for the 2026 Deloitte Graduate and Vacationer Programs, providing university students and recent graduates with the chance to develop future-ready skills, gain hands-on experience, and begin a rewarding professional journey with one of the world’s […]

The post Deloitte Graduate Program 2026 Applications Open: Launch Your Career with One of the World’s Leading Professional Services Firms appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Every successful career starts with the right opportunity, and Deloitte is offering aspiring professionals exactly that. Applications are now open for the 2026 Deloitte Graduate and Vacationer Programs, providing university students and recent graduates with the chance to develop future-ready skills, gain hands-on experience, and begin a rewarding professional journey with one of the world’s leading consulting and professional services organizations.

Whether you aspire to work in consulting, technology, audit, tax, strategy, engineering, or other professional disciplines, Deloitte’s Graduate Program is designed to help talented individuals build meaningful careers while making a real impact from their very first day.

Applications are open from 1 July 2026 until 22 July 2026, so interested candidates are encouraged to apply before the deadline.

About the Deloitte Graduate Program

The Deloitte Graduate Program is designed for students who are completing their university studies during mid-to-late 2026 or graduates who have completed their degrees within the previous two years. Successful applicants will begin their careers with Deloitte in 2027.

From the moment graduates join Deloitte, they become part of a diverse cohort of professionals representing various academic disciplines, backgrounds, and experiences. The program provides an inclusive environment where participants collaborate, learn together, and establish lasting professional relationships.

Unlike many graduate programs that limit applicants based on their academic background, Deloitte recognizes that talent comes in many forms. Graduates are encouraged to explore different career pathways regardless of their degree specialization.

For example, Deloitte welcomes:

  • Technology graduates working in Tax
  • Engineers contributing to Audit
  • IT graduates joining Strategy
  • Professionals from various disciplines across multiple business units

Your university degree serves as a starting point rather than a limitation.

Why Choose Deloitte?

Deloitte believes that exceptional careers are built in environments where employees are trusted, supported, and inspired to perform at their best.

Successful graduates will enjoy numerous benefits throughout their professional journey.

1. Make an Impact from Day One

Rather than spending months observing, graduates immediately contribute to meaningful projects that create value for clients, businesses, and communities.

Participants gain practical experience while working alongside experienced professionals on real business challenges.

2. Build Future-Ready Skills

The program equips graduates with practical and professional skills required to succeed in today’s rapidly changing business environment.

Participants strengthen competencies such as:

  • Problem-solving
  • Communication
  • Leadership
  • Collaboration
  • Innovation
  • Critical thinking
  • Client engagement
  • Professional consulting skills

3. Graduate Learning Academy

One of the highlights of the program is Deloitte’s Graduate Learning Academy.

This structured learning experience includes:

  • Interactive workshops
  • Creative challenges
  • Practical learning activities
  • Professional coaching
  • Skill development sessions
  • Confidence-building exercises
  • Networking opportunities

The academy helps graduates establish strong foundations for long-term career success.

4. Dedicated Career Coaching

Every graduate receives personalized career support through:

  • A dedicated career coach
  • Professional mentoring
  • Peer support networks
  • Continuous feedback
  • Career guidance
  • Personal development planning

Graduates are never expected to navigate their professional journey alone.

5. Continuous Learning Opportunities

Learning continues beyond onboarding.

Participants have access to:

  • E-learning platforms
  • Professional development resources
  • Ongoing mentoring
  • Skills enhancement programs
  • Career progression opportunities

This commitment to continuous learning enables graduates to adapt and thrive throughout their careers.

6. Explore Diverse Career Paths

Deloitte offers opportunities across numerous industries and business areas, allowing graduates to discover where their strengths and interests align.

Participants can explore multiple professional pathways while building deep technical and industry expertise.

Who Can Apply?

The Graduate Program is intended for applicants who meet one of the following criteria:

  • Students completing university studies between mid-2026 and the end of 2026.
  • Graduates who completed their degree within the last two years.
  • Individuals ready to begin a graduate career with Deloitte in 2027.

Applicants from a wide variety of academic disciplines are encouraged to apply.

What Makes Deloitte Different?

Deloitte values diversity of thought, education, and experience.

Rather than restricting career paths based on university degrees, Deloitte encourages graduates to pursue opportunities that match their interests, skills, and potential.

This flexible approach allows graduates to develop careers that align with both their professional aspirations and evolving business needs.

Skills Matcher Quiz

Applicants who are unsure which Deloitte team best suits their interests can complete Deloitte’s Skills Matcher Quiz.

The quiz helps candidates identify career opportunities that align with their:

  • Skills
  • Interests
  • Preferences
  • Career aspirations

This makes it easier to choose the most suitable graduate pathway before submitting an application.

Application Deadline

Applications open: 1 July 2026

Application deadline: 22 July 2026

Interested applicants should complete and submit their applications before the closing date.

How to Apply

Prospective applicants should:

  1. Explore Deloitte’s available Graduate and Vacationer opportunities.
  2. Learn about the different business areas and roles.
  3. Complete the Skills Matcher Quiz if needed.
  4. Submit an online application before 22 July 2026.

Click here for more and to apply

Starting your career with Deloitte offers far more than a graduate job—it provides an opportunity to learn, grow, build meaningful professional relationships, and contribute to impactful work while developing a successful long-term career.

Click here for more global internship opportunities

The post Deloitte Graduate Program 2026 Applications Open: Launch Your Career with One of the World’s Leading Professional Services Firms appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator 2026: Receive Up to AUD 840,000 (Approximately USD 550,000) Plus Expert Acceleration Support to Scale Climate and Gender Equality Solutions Across the Indo-Pacific
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Organizations working to solve climate change and gender inequality often face one common challenge—accessing sufficient financing to scale their impact. While grants remain important, attracting private investment has become increasingly critical for achieving sustainable development goals at scale. To address this challenge, the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT), in partnership with Convergence […]

The post Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator 2026: Receive Up to AUD 840,000 (Approximately USD 550,000) Plus Expert Acceleration Support to Scale Climate and Gender Equality Solutions Across the Indo-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to solve climate change and gender inequality often face one common challenge—accessing sufficient financing to scale their impact. While grants remain important, attracting private investment has become increasingly critical for achieving sustainable development goals at scale.

To address this challenge, the Australian Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT), in partnership with Convergence Blended Finance, has launched the Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator, a five-year initiative worth AUD 10 million. The programme is designed to help NGOs develop innovative blended finance solutions that unlock private investment for climate action and gender equality initiatives throughout the Indo-Pacific region.

The Accelerator combines catalytic grant funding with technical acceleration support, enabling NGOs to transform innovative ideas into investment-ready financial vehicles capable of delivering measurable environmental and social impact.

Application Deadline: 20 July 2026

What is the Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator?

The Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator is a comprehensive programme that helps NGOs bridge the gap between development funding and private capital.

Although blended finance has expanded significantly across the Indo-Pacific, there remains a shortage of investable projects capable of attracting commercial investors. NGOs possess deep local knowledge, technical expertise, and trusted community relationships, making them well positioned to design impactful financial solutions. However, they currently sponsor only a small percentage of blended finance transactions globally due to several institutional and financial barriers.

The Accelerator seeks to change this by providing financial support, expert guidance, investor connections, and technical resources that enable NGOs to create scalable blended finance vehicles capable of attracting long-term investment.

Total Programme Funding

The programme is backed by a five-year AUD 10 million investment and provides both financial support and capacity-building services.

Available catalytic grants include:

  • Up to AUD 300,000 (approximately USD 200,000) for early-stage blended finance initiatives.
  • Up to AUD 840,000 (approximately USD 550,000) for advanced-stage and scaling initiatives.

All grants are disbursed in Australian Dollars (AUD).

What is Blended Finance?

Blended finance refers to the strategic use of public, philanthropic, or concessional funding to attract private investment into projects that generate both financial returns and positive development outcomes.

Rather than relying solely on donor funding, blended finance combines different sources of capital to create sustainable financing mechanisms capable of delivering large-scale impact.

Examples include:

  • Investment funds
  • Project finance vehicles
  • Private companies
  • Bonds
  • Financing facilities
  • Results-based financing mechanisms
  • Other innovative financial instruments

Programme Objectives

The Accelerator aims to:

  • Increase private investment for climate action.
  • Strengthen financing for gender equality initiatives.
  • Build a pipeline of investment-ready blended finance projects.
  • Support NGOs in becoming investment managers and impact managers.
  • Expand partnerships between NGOs and private investors.
  • Deliver measurable environmental and social outcomes.
  • Strengthen financial sustainability for development initiatives.

Priority Impact Areas

Applications should focus on one or both of the programme’s major themes.

Climate Action

Projects may support:

  • Climate change mitigation
  • Climate adaptation
  • Clean energy
  • Sustainable cities
  • Responsible consumption
  • Life below water
  • Life on land

These areas align with Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs):

  • SDG 7 – Affordable and Clean Energy
  • SDG 11 – Sustainable Cities and Communities
  • SDG 12 – Responsible Consumption and Production
  • SDG 13 – Climate Action
  • SDG 14 – Life Below Water
  • SDG 15 – Life on Land

Gender Equality

Projects should demonstrate a strong commitment to gender equality through:

  • Women’s empowerment
  • Financial inclusion
  • Healthcare
  • Education
  • Gender-responsive investment models
  • Gender integration throughout project design, implementation, and governance

Applications should align with SDG 5 – Gender Equality.

What Support Will Selected NGOs Receive?

Successful applicants receive more than funding.

The programme provides:

1. Catalytic Grant Funding

Funding supports activities such as:

  • Feasibility studies
  • Legal structuring
  • Financial modelling
  • Vehicle design
  • Investment pipeline development
  • Fundraising
  • Pilot implementation
  • Governance development
  • Impact measurement systems

2. Acceleration Support

Participants also gain access to:

  • Professional training
  • Curated market intelligence
  • Data and analytics
  • Technical mentoring
  • Peer-learning opportunities
  • Investor introductions
  • Ecosystem partnerships
  • Ongoing feedback from blended finance experts
  • Guidance on engaging commercial investors

Types of Grants Available

Stage 1: Scoping and Feasibility

Maximum funding:

  • Up to AUD 300,000

Suitable for NGOs that are exploring blended finance opportunities.

Funding may support:

  • Financial model development
  • Vehicle design
  • Legal structuring
  • Market assessments
  • Stakeholder consultations
  • Investment pipeline development
  • Governance framework design
  • Initial impact assessment

Applicants should already have:

  • An initial theory of change
  • Early scoping work
  • Potential partners identified

Stage 2: Proof of Concept and Pilot

Maximum funding:

  • Up to AUD 840,000

Funding supports:

  • Investor outreach
  • Legal refinement
  • Governance improvements
  • Impact framework development
  • Pilot investments
  • Investment pipeline expansion

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Completed feasibility work
  • Initial financial model
  • Internal and external support
  • Key implementation partners
  • At least one soft or hard investor commitment

Stage 3: Scale-Up and Expansion

Maximum funding:

  • Up to AUD 840,000

Funding may be used for:

  • Vehicle expansion
  • Operational scaling
  • Additional fundraising
  • Legal improvements
  • Pipeline growth
  • General Partner (GP) capital commitments
  • Preparing vehicles for larger investment rounds

Applicants should already have:

  • An investment-ready or operational vehicle
  • Anchor investors
  • Committed implementation partners
  • Defined NGO roles

Eligible Financial Vehicles

Projects may include:

  • Investment funds
  • Bonds
  • Project finance structures
  • Private companies
  • Financing facilities
  • Results-based financing mechanisms
  • Other innovative blended finance instruments

Countries Eligible for Project Implementation

Projects must focus on one or more eligible Indo-Pacific countries, including:

  • Bangladesh
  • Bhutan
  • Cambodia
  • Indonesia
  • Laos
  • Mongolia
  • Myanmar
  • Nepal
  • Pakistan
  • Philippines
  • Sri Lanka
  • Timor-Leste
  • Vietnam

Eligible Pacific Island nations include:

  • Cook Islands
  • Federated States of Micronesia
  • Fiji
  • Kiribati
  • Nauru
  • Niue
  • Papua New Guinea
  • Republic of Palau
  • Republic of the Marshall Islands
  • Samoa
  • Solomon Islands
  • Tokelau
  • Tonga
  • Tuvalu
  • Vanuatu

Applicants may be based anywhere in the world, provided their proposed blended finance vehicle serves eligible Indo-Pacific countries.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be an NGO applying independently or with consortium partners.
  • Demonstrate a clear need for grant funding.
  • Develop a blended finance solution capable of attracting private investment.
  • Hold a significant implementation role.
  • Obtain internal leadership approval.
  • Target eligible Indo-Pacific countries.
  • Focus on climate action, gender equality, or both.
  • Present a credible pathway toward investment readiness and financial sustainability.

Evaluation Criteria

Applications will be assessed based on:

Development Impact

Projects should generate measurable environmental and social benefits, particularly for underserved populations including:

  • Women
  • Indigenous communities
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Communities in Least Developed Countries (LDCs)
  • Frontier markets

Scalability and Replicability

Proposals should demonstrate potential to:

  • Mobilize significant private investment
  • Expand across multiple markets
  • Serve as models for future blended finance initiatives

Additionality

Applicants should explain:

  • Why blended finance is necessary
  • How the project addresses financing gaps
  • Why grant funding is essential

Gender Integration

Strong proposals integrate gender considerations throughout project design rather than treating gender as an additional feature.

Investment Readiness

Projects should demonstrate:

  • Strong governance
  • Financial sustainability
  • Investor engagement
  • Commercial viability
  • Risk management

Internal Organizational Commitment

Applicants should show:

  • Leadership support
  • Dedicated implementation teams
  • Alignment with organizational strategy

Stakeholder Partnerships

Preference is given to proposals with:

  • Financial partners
  • Implementation partners
  • Regional stakeholder support

Track Record

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • Relevant technical expertise
  • Local implementation experience
  • Strong partnerships
  • Capacity to manage blended finance initiatives

Why This Programme Matters

Traditional grant funding alone cannot meet the growing financing needs for climate resilience and gender equality across the Indo-Pacific. By combining grants with technical expertise and private investment, the Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator enables NGOs to create sustainable financing solutions that continue generating impact long after grant funding has ended.

The programme also strengthens NGO capacity to engage commercial investors, manage innovative financial vehicles, and build scalable development solutions capable of transforming communities across the region.

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: 20 July 2026

Interested NGOs are encouraged to prepare their concept notes well before the deadline to maximize their chances of success.

Click here for more and to apply

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post Indo-Pacific NGO Blended Finance Accelerator 2026: Receive Up to AUD 840,000 (Approximately USD 550,000) Plus Expert Acceleration Support to Scale Climate and Gender Equality Solutions Across the Indo-Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GreenLight Fund Expression of Interest 2026: Receive Up to US$600,000 in Unrestricted Funding to Expand Your Nonprofit Across the United States
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
Nonprofit organizations with proven, evidence-based programs often reach a stage where their impact has been demonstrated, but expanding into new communities requires significant financial investment, strategic planning, staffing support, and strong local partnerships. GreenLight Fund is addressing this challenge by inviting eligible U.S.-based nonprofit organizations to submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) for future funding […]

The post GreenLight Fund Expression of Interest 2026: Receive Up to US$600,000 in Unrestricted Funding to Expand Your Nonprofit Across the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Nonprofit organizations with proven, evidence-based programs often reach a stage where their impact has been demonstrated, but expanding into new communities requires significant financial investment, strategic planning, staffing support, and strong local partnerships. GreenLight Fund is addressing this challenge by inviting eligible U.S.-based nonprofit organizations to submit an Expression of Interest (EOI) for future funding and expansion opportunities.

Through this initiative, selected organizations may receive up to US$600,000 in unrestricted funding over four years, together with comprehensive strategic support designed to help successful nonprofit models launch and thrive in new communities throughout the United States.

Unlike many traditional grant opportunities that provide funding alone, GreenLight Fund combines long-term financial investment with practical implementation support through its nationally recognized “Beyond the Check” partnership model.

Expression of Interest Deadline

Expression of Interest Pilot Deadline: July 17, 2026

Organizations interested in joining GreenLight Fund’s future partnership pipeline should submit their Expression of Interest before the deadline.

It is important to note that submitting an Expression of Interest is not the same as submitting a full grant application. Rather, it allows GreenLight Fund to learn more about your organization and determine whether it aligns with current or future partnership opportunities.

Organizations whose missions and programs align with GreenLight Fund’s model may later be invited into additional review and selection stages.

About GreenLight Fund

GreenLight Fund is a national nonprofit organization that works alongside local communities across the United States to improve opportunities for inclusive prosperity.

Rather than imposing predetermined solutions, GreenLight partners directly with communities to identify their most pressing unmet needs before selecting proven nonprofit organizations capable of addressing those challenges.

The organization focuses on removing barriers that prevent individuals and families from achieving long-term economic mobility, particularly those rooted in racial and economic inequities.

Today, GreenLight operates across multiple sites throughout the United States and continues expanding its national presence.

Each year, every GreenLight site follows a structured community-driven process to identify one critical local challenge and invest in a nonprofit organization capable of delivering measurable and sustainable results.

Funding Benefits

Organizations selected into GreenLight Fund’s portfolio receive far more than financial support.

Successful nonprofits may receive:

  • Up to US$600,000 in unrestricted funding over four years.
  • Multi-year investment designed to support long-term growth.
  • Strategic guidance throughout expansion into new communities.
  • Access to GreenLight Fund’s “Beyond the Check” support model.
  • Coaching on organizational strategy.
  • Leadership and staffing support.
  • Assistance overcoming implementation challenges.
  • Partnership development with local organizations.
  • Connections to additional funding opportunities.
  • Access to GreenLight’s national network of community leaders.
  • Support from experienced local advisory boards.
  • Ongoing relationship-building with stakeholders.
  • Assistance developing sustainable local operations.
  • Long-term accountability and performance measurement.

Because the funding is unrestricted, organizations have greater flexibility to invest resources where they are most needed to achieve successful expansion.

What Makes GreenLight Fund Different?

Many grant programs simply award funding and expect organizations to implement projects independently.

GreenLight Fund takes a different approach.

Through its “Beyond the Check” model, GreenLight works as a long-term strategic partner throughout the expansion journey.

This includes:

  • Strategic coaching
  • Local relationship building
  • Staffing guidance
  • Partnership development
  • Fundraising support
  • Leadership mentoring
  • Sustainability planning
  • Performance measurement

The objective is not only to help nonprofits launch in a new city but also to ensure they become sustainable, community-supported organizations capable of creating lasting impact.

Priority Impact Areas

Although each GreenLight community identifies its own local priorities annually, the organization commonly supports nonprofit solutions addressing:

  • Housing stability
  • Education
  • Youth opportunity
  • Workforce development
  • Economic mobility
  • Health and well-being
  • Family support
  • Community resilience
  • Reducing racial disparities
  • Reducing economic disparities
  • Inclusive prosperity initiatives

Organizations operating in one or more of these focus areas are encouraged to explore the opportunity.

Who Should Apply?

GreenLight Fund is seeking nonprofit organizations that are prepared to scale successful programs into additional communities.

Strong candidates typically:

  • Operate as U.S.-based nonprofit organizations.
  • Have a proven, evidence-based model.
  • Demonstrate measurable outcomes.
  • Can clearly show the impact of their work.
  • Are interested in expanding into new U.S. communities.
  • Address challenges related to economic mobility and inclusive prosperity.
  • Have leadership committed to long-term growth and partnership.

Organizations still testing early-stage ideas or pilot programs may not yet be ready for this opportunity, as GreenLight is specifically looking for solutions with demonstrated success that can be replicated effectively in new locations.

Understanding the GreenLight Method

GreenLight Fund follows a structured five-step process to identify and scale nonprofit solutions.

1. Discover

GreenLight works closely with local communities, advisory committees, residents, and stakeholders to identify unmet community needs using research, local data, and extensive listening sessions.

2. Scout

Once priority challenges have been identified, GreenLight searches nationally for nonprofit organizations with proven models capable of addressing those needs.

3. Select

Potential organizations undergo careful evaluation before one nonprofit is selected as the strongest local fit.

4. Invest

Selected organizations receive unrestricted funding together with strategic support to establish and expand operations within the new community.

5. Measure

GreenLight works with partner organizations to establish measurable goals and track both programmatic and financial performance throughout the partnership.

This structured approach helps ensure that investments produce measurable outcomes and long-term community impact.

The GreenLight Partnership Journey

Organizations interested in partnering with GreenLight Fund can expect several stages throughout the selection process.

Stage 1: Interest and Exploration

Organizations submit an Expression of Interest indicating their desire to be considered for future partnership opportunities.

Stage 2: Pipeline Qualification

GreenLight reviews submitted information to determine whether the organization aligns with its partnership model.

Qualified organizations may be invited to an introductory conversation.

Stage 3: Diligence and Alignment

Organizations may be asked to provide additional documentation and participate in deeper discussions regarding program effectiveness, expansion readiness, and organizational capacity.

Stage 4: Community-Driven Selection

Finalists may present their work before a Selection Advisory Committee and engage directly with local community stakeholders.

Stage 5: Partnership and Investment

Each year, GreenLight selects one organization per site to expand into a new community.

Selected organizations enter a formal partnership agreement outlining funding, expectations, strategic support, and shared accountability.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Scaling nonprofit programs successfully requires much more than financial investment.

Organizations often need:

  • Strong local partnerships.
  • Community trust.
  • Leadership coaching.
  • Sustainable staffing structures.
  • Fundraising guidance.
  • Strategic planning.
  • Long-term implementation support.

GreenLight Fund addresses all of these needs simultaneously through its integrated funding and partnership approach.

For nonprofits with proven models ready to reach more communities, this opportunity offers one of the most comprehensive expansion support programs currently available in the United States.

Important Notes

  • Expression of Interest Deadline: July 17, 2026
  • Submitting an Expression of Interest does not constitute a formal application.
  • GreenLight Fund staff will review submissions to determine organizational alignment.
  • Organizations that demonstrate strong alignment may be contacted for additional conversations or future selection cycles.
  • Selection is based on community priorities, organizational readiness, measurable impact, and alignment with GreenLight Fund’s mission.

How to Apply

Eligible nonprofit organizations should complete the GreenLight Fund Expression of Interest Form before the July 17, 2026 deadline.

Click here for more and to apply

Click here for more global grant opportunities

The post GreenLight Fund Expression of Interest 2026: Receive Up to US$600,000 in Unrestricted Funding to Expand Your Nonprofit Across the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Free SOUTHMOD Training Course 2026 on Tax and Benefit Microsimulation for Latin America | Hybrid Participation in Quito, Ecuador
item.dc:creator: Grace Amo
item.description:
Professionals, researchers, policymakers, and academics interested in tax and social protection policy analysis are invited to register for the SOUTHMOD Training Course on Microsimulation for Tax and Benefit Analysis in Latin America, taking place 14–16 July 2026 in Quito, Ecuador. Organized by the United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) in collaboration […]

The post Free SOUTHMOD Training Course 2026 on Tax and Benefit Microsimulation for Latin America | Hybrid Participation in Quito, Ecuador appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Professionals, researchers, policymakers, and academics interested in tax and social protection policy analysis are invited to register for the SOUTHMOD Training Course on Microsimulation for Tax and Benefit Analysis in Latin America, taking place 14–16 July 2026 in Quito, Ecuador.

Organized by the United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER) in collaboration with Facultad Latinoamericana de Ciencias Sociales (FLACSO Ecuador), Universidad del Pacífico, the International Inequalities Institute at the London School of Economics (LSE), and Pontificia Universidad Javeriana’s Observatorio Fiscal, this three-day hybrid training programme provides participants with practical skills in using tax-benefit microsimulation models to evaluate fiscal policies.

The training is completely free of charge, open to all eligible participants, and will be delivered entirely in Spanish.

Registration Status

Registration is currently open.

Training Dates

14 July 2026 – 16 July 2026

Venue

Quito, Ecuador

The course will be offered in a hybrid format, allowing participants to attend either in person or through virtual participation.

About the SOUTHMOD Training Course

The SOUTHMOD training course is designed to equip participants with practical knowledge and hands-on experience in using microsimulation models to analyze the impact of tax and social benefit policies across Latin America.

Participants will learn how to evaluate how different fiscal policies affect:

  • Household income
  • Poverty levels
  • Income inequality
  • Government revenues
  • Social protection outcomes
  • Distribution of taxes and benefits

The programme focuses on practical applications that support evidence-based policymaking and economic research.

What is SOUTHMOD?

SOUTHMOD is an internationally recognized research initiative that develops, updates, and maintains tax-benefit microsimulation models for low- and middle-income countries.

These models combine:

  • Household income data
  • Household expenditure data
  • National tax legislation
  • Social benefit policies

Using these datasets, researchers and policymakers can simulate proposed policy changes before implementation and estimate their likely economic and social impacts.

This makes SOUTHMOD an essential tool for governments, researchers, and development organizations seeking to design more effective fiscal and social protection policies.

Countries Covered by the Training

This training focuses on four Latin American microsimulation models:

  • ECUAMOD (Ecuador)
  • COLMOD (Colombia)
  • BOLMOD (Bolivia)
  • PERUMOD (Peru)

Participants will gain practical experience using these country-specific models to assess tax and benefit reforms.

What Participants Will Learn

Throughout the three-day programme, participants will receive practical instruction on how to:

  • Use tax-benefit microsimulation software.
  • Analyze household-level income and expenditure data.
  • Evaluate fiscal policy reforms.
  • Estimate poverty impacts.
  • Measure inequality outcomes.
  • Assess government revenue implications.
  • Compare alternative tax and social benefit scenarios.
  • Interpret simulation results for policymaking.

The course emphasizes applied learning through real-world examples and hands-on exercises.

Who Should Attend?

The programme is intended for both experienced and first-time SOUTHMOD users.

Ideal participants include:

  • Government officials involved in fiscal policy.
  • Public finance specialists.
  • Social policy analysts.
  • Academic researchers.
  • University lecturers.
  • Think tank professionals.
  • Public institution analysts.
  • Graduate students interested in tax and social policy.

Anyone interested in evidence-based policymaking and fiscal analysis is encouraged to participate.

Why Tax-Benefit Microsimulation Matters

Tax-benefit microsimulation has become one of the most valuable tools for governments and researchers because it enables policymakers to test the likely consequences of policy reforms before implementation.

These models help answer important policy questions, including:

  • How will a proposed tax reform affect low-income households?
  • Which social benefit programme is most effective at reducing poverty?
  • What impact will policy changes have on government revenues?
  • How will inequality change under different fiscal scenarios?

Such evidence supports smarter, fairer, and more efficient public policy decisions.

Global Reach of SOUTHMOD

UNU-WIDER currently hosts fourteen tax-benefit microsimulation models covering countries across multiple regions.

Africa

  • Egypt
  • Ethiopia
  • Ghana
  • Mozambique
  • Rwanda
  • Tanzania
  • Uganda
  • Zambia

Latin America

  • Bolivia
  • Colombia
  • Ecuador
  • Peru

Southeast Asia

  • Viet Nam

Other

  • Zanzibar

These models complement previously developed systems for South Africa and Namibia, making SOUTHMOD one of the world’s leading tax-benefit microsimulation initiatives for developing economies.

Organizing Institutions

The training course is jointly organized by:

  • United Nations University World Institute for Development Economics Research (UNU-WIDER)
  • Facultad Latinoamericana de Ciencias Sociales (FLACSO Ecuador)
  • Universidad del Pacífico
  • International Inequalities Institute, London School of Economics (LSE)
  • Pontificia Universidad Javeriana – Observatorio Fiscal

This collaboration brings together leading experts in economics, public finance, social policy, and fiscal analysis.

Benefits of Participating

Participants will benefit from:

  • Free professional training.
  • Practical hands-on learning.
  • Hybrid participation options.
  • Exposure to internationally recognized policy tools.
  • Improved analytical skills.
  • Better understanding of tax-benefit modelling.
  • Networking opportunities with researchers and policymakers.
  • Knowledge applicable to fiscal policy design and evaluation.
  • Enhanced research and policy analysis capabilities.

Why You Should Register

Whether you work in government, academia, public policy, or development research, this course offers an excellent opportunity to strengthen your expertise in tax-benefit microsimulation and evidence-based fiscal policymaking.

By learning to use SOUTHMOD, participants will gain practical skills that can support better policy design, improve social protection systems, and contribute to reducing poverty and inequality across developing countries.

Registration is free, and interested participants are encouraged to secure their place before the programme begins on 14 July 2026.

Apply Here

For more information on this training opportunity visit here 

Discover more training opportunities on OFY here

The post Free SOUTHMOD Training Course 2026 on Tax and Benefit Microsimulation for Latin America | Hybrid Participation in Quito, Ecuador appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: European Studies Review Job Fair 2026: Discover the Latest Internships, Traineeships, and Career Opportunities Across Europe
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Finding the right internship, traineeship, or entry-level job can be one of the most important steps toward building a successful international career. The European Studies Review (ESR) Job Fair brings together some of the latest opportunities offered by leading European institutions, international organizations, consulting firms, research institutes, and private-sector employers. Updated regularly and promoted every […]

The post European Studies Review Job Fair 2026: Discover the Latest Internships, Traineeships, and Career Opportunities Across Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Finding the right internship, traineeship, or entry-level job can be one of the most important steps toward building a successful international career. The European Studies Review (ESR) Job Fair brings together some of the latest opportunities offered by leading European institutions, international organizations, consulting firms, research institutes, and private-sector employers.

Updated regularly and promoted every Wednesday on LinkedIn, the ESR Job Fair serves as a valuable resource for students, graduates, and young professionals seeking career opportunities in diplomacy, European Union affairs, economics, law, communications, finance, sustainability, research, and public policy.

Whether you are looking to gain your first professional experience, develop specialized skills, or begin a long-term career within European institutions, this collection of opportunities provides an excellent starting point.

Why Follow the European Studies Review Job Fair?

The European Studies Review has become a trusted platform for promoting opportunities that help young professionals launch successful international careers.

The Job Fair features vacancies from prestigious organizations across Europe, including European Union institutions, international organizations, consulting firms, financial institutions, research centers, and advocacy organizations.

Applicants benefit from having multiple career opportunities available in one place, allowing them to explore positions that match their education, interests, and professional goals.

Latest Opportunities Available

1. UNITAR – Multilateral Diplomacy Programme Traineeship

Location: Geneva, Switzerland

The United Nations Institute for Training and Research (UNITAR) is recruiting trainees for its Multilateral Diplomacy Programme.

This opportunity provides participants with practical exposure to international diplomacy while supporting UNITAR’s work in multilateral cooperation, global governance, and diplomatic capacity-building.

Application Deadline: 8 July

 

2. European Central Bank – Financial Analysis Traineeship

Location: Frankfurt am Main, Germany

The European Central Bank (ECB) is offering a traineeship within the Directorate General Market Infrastructure and Payments.

Successful candidates will gain valuable experience in financial analysis while working alongside experts responsible for Europe’s financial infrastructure and payment systems.

Application Deadline: 9 July

 

3. European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) – Multiple Traineeships

Location: Paris, France

ESMA is offering multiple traineeship positions for graduates interested in financial regulation, market supervision, and European financial governance.

Participants will work alongside specialists responsible for maintaining the stability and integrity of European financial markets.

Application Deadline: 15 July

 

4. European Economic and Social Committee – Traineeship Programme

The European Economic and Social Committee offers traineeships that provide participants with practical experience in European policymaking while working within one of the European Union’s advisory institutions.

The programme allows trainees to understand how EU institutions operate and contribute to policy development.

Application Deadline: 30 September

 

5. European Committee of the Regions – Traineeships

The European Committee of the Regions offers traineeships for graduates interested in regional development, European governance, and local policymaking.

Participants gain first-hand experience supporting one of the European Union’s consultative bodies.

Application Deadline: 30 September

 

6. British Chamber of Commerce (EU & Belgium) – Committees Officer

This position offers professionals an opportunity to support committee activities, stakeholder engagement, and organizational coordination within the British Chamber of Commerce.

Application Deadline: 3 July

 

7. Avocats Sans Frontières – Research Assistant Intern

Applicants interested in legal research, human rights, and international justice can apply for the Research Assistant Internship offered by Avocats Sans Frontières.

The internship provides valuable practical experience supporting research activities within an international legal organization.

Application Deadline: 12 July

 

8. Time&Place Consulting – Advocacy & Communication Intern

Time&Place Consulting is recruiting an Advocacy and Communication Intern.

Successful applicants will gain hands-on experience supporting advocacy initiatives, strategic communication, stakeholder engagement, and policy communication.

Application Deadline: 15 July

 

9. EIPA – EU Policy-Making Intern

The European Institute of Public Administration (EIPA) offers an internship focused on European Union policy-making.

Participants gain practical knowledge of EU governance, institutional processes, and policy implementation.

Application Deadline: 15 July

 

10. EGMONT – Royal Institute for International Relations

The Royal Institute for International Relations is offering internships within its Education and Training Programme.

This opportunity is ideal for candidates interested in international affairs, diplomacy, security studies, and foreign policy.

Application Deadline: 15 July

 

11. EUnited – Membership & Communications Officer

EUnited is recruiting a Membership and Communications Officer to support member engagement, communications strategies, and organizational activities.

Application Deadline: 31 July

 

12. EY Belgium – Internship Payroll

EY Belgium offers internship opportunities within its payroll team.

Participants gain professional experience working within one of the world’s leading professional services firms.

Application Deadline: Rolling applications

 

13. Acumen Public Affairs – Trainee Consultant Opportunities

Acumen Public Affairs is recruiting:

  • Health Trainee Consultant
  • Sustainability Trainee Consultant

These opportunities allow graduates to gain consulting experience within health policy and sustainability sectors.

Application Deadline: Rolling applications

 

Benefits of Following the ESR Job Fair

Applicants can benefit from:

  • Access to multiple internship, traineeship, and job opportunities in one place.
  • Opportunities with prestigious European institutions and international organizations.
  • Career development in diplomacy, finance, communications, public policy, law, economics, sustainability, and international relations.
  • Practical professional experience.
  • Exposure to European policymaking and governance.
  • Networking opportunities with leading professionals.
  • A convenient weekly source of newly published career opportunities.

Why You Should Apply

Many of these organizations are internationally recognized and provide excellent environments for learning, professional development, and long-term career growth. Whether your ambition is to work within European Union institutions, international organizations, financial institutions, research institutes, or consulting firms, these opportunities can serve as important stepping stones toward achieving your career goals.

Application Deadlines

The opportunities featured in this Job Fair have different application deadlines:

  • British Chamber of Commerce – 3 July
  • UNITAR – 8 July
  • European Central Bank – 9 July
  • Avocats Sans Frontières – 12 July
  • ESMA – 15 July
  • Time&Place Consulting – 15 July
  • EIPA – 15 July
  • EGMONT – 15 July
  • EUnited – 31 July
  • European Economic and Social Committee – 30 September
  • European Committee of the Regions – 30 September
  • EY Belgium – Rolling Deadline
  • Acumen Public Affairs – Rolling Deadline

Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications well before the stated deadlines.

How to Stay Updated

European Studies Review publishes new career opportunities every Wednesday through its Job Fair.

Follow the platform to stay informed about internships, traineeships, graduate programmes, fellowships, and job openings across Europe.

Official Website:

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post European Studies Review Job Fair 2026: Discover the Latest Internships, Traineeships, and Career Opportunities Across Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Bank of America Campus Experience 2026: Launch Your Career with World-Class Internships, Training, Mentorship, and Global Networking Opportunities
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
Starting your professional career with one of the world’s leading financial institutions can significantly shape your future. The Bank of America Campus Experience offers students and early-career professionals an outstanding opportunity to gain real-world experience, receive mentorship from industry experts, develop leadership skills, and build a strong professional network within one of the most respected […]

The post Bank of America Campus Experience 2026: Launch Your Career with World-Class Internships, Training, Mentorship, and Global Networking Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Starting your professional career with one of the world’s leading financial institutions can significantly shape your future. The Bank of America Campus Experience offers students and early-career professionals an outstanding opportunity to gain real-world experience, receive mentorship from industry experts, develop leadership skills, and build a strong professional network within one of the most respected global banking institutions.

Whether you are looking for an internship or your first full-time position after graduation, Bank of America provides a structured career development experience designed to help young professionals transition successfully from academic life into the corporate world.

If you’re ambitious, driven, eager to learn, and ready to challenge yourself, the Bank of America Campus Experience could be the perfect opportunity to begin your career.

Why Choose the Bank of America Campus Experience?

Beginning a career is about much more than simply finding a job. It is about joining an organization that invests in your long-term growth and provides you with the tools necessary to succeed.

At Bank of America, students are welcomed into a collaborative environment where learning, innovation, and professional development are prioritized from day one.

The Campus Experience is carefully designed to provide participants with meaningful work assignments while supporting their personal and professional development throughout the program.

What Makes the Campus Experience Unique?

Participants enjoy access to a wide range of career development resources, including:

1. Supportive Mentorship

Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals working in their chosen field of interest.

Mentors help students:

  • Understand the banking industry
  • Develop professional skills
  • Navigate workplace challenges
  • Build confidence
  • Explore future career opportunities

Having access to experienced professionals allows participants to accelerate their learning and gain valuable career advice.

2. Industry-Leading Training

Bank of America complements classroom education with practical learning experiences.

Participants receive training that helps them:

  • Strengthen technical knowledge
  • Improve business communication
  • Develop analytical thinking
  • Build leadership capabilities
  • Learn industry best practices
  • Prepare for long-term career success

The goal is to help students develop skills that extend far beyond what they learned in school.

3. Strong Culture and Community

The Campus Experience encourages collaboration and relationship-building among interns and early-career professionals.

Participants become part of a cohort of fellow students where they can:

  • Learn together
  • Collaborate on projects
  • Share experiences
  • Build lasting friendships
  • Support one another throughout the program

This collaborative environment helps create an engaging and enjoyable workplace experience.

4. Extensive Networking Opportunities

Networking is an essential part of career growth.

Bank of America gives participants opportunities to connect with professionals from across the organization.

Networking opportunities include:

  • Business leaders
  • Experienced professionals
  • Fellow interns
  • Graduate hires
  • Cross-functional teams
  • Company-wide events

These connections can become valuable professional relationships throughout your career.

5. Award-Winning Onboarding Experience

New participants receive a welcoming orientation designed to make the transition into the workplace as smooth as possible.

The onboarding process introduces participants to:

  • Company values
  • Workplace expectations
  • Available resources
  • Career development opportunities
  • Professional support systems

This helps new hires feel confident and prepared from the beginning.

6. Inclusive Workplace Environment

Bank of America promotes an inclusive culture where every employee is encouraged to bring their authentic self to work.

The organization values diversity and seeks to create an environment where everyone feels respected, supported, and able to contribute meaningfully.

7. Exclusive Events for Early Career Professionals

Students participating in the Campus Experience can enjoy events specifically organized for interns and entry-level employees.

These activities provide opportunities for learning, networking, collaboration, and engagement beyond everyday work responsibilities.

8. Additional Employee Perks

Participants may also enjoy various unique experiences throughout the program, including:

  • Social gatherings
  • Team-building activities
  • Fun giveaways
  • Community events
  • Special celebrations

These activities contribute to a positive workplace culture while helping participants build relationships across the organization.

Who Should Apply?

The Campus Experience is designed for students and recent graduates who are eager to launch successful careers within the financial services industry.

Ideal candidates are individuals who are:

  • Motivated
  • Hardworking
  • Curious
  • Ambitious
  • Ready to learn
  • Interested in professional growth
  • Comfortable working in collaborative environments

Career Opportunities Available

Bank of America offers both:

  • Internship opportunities
  • Entry-level positions

Available roles vary by country, business area, and recruitment cycle.

Applicants are encouraged to explore opportunities available within their region.

Why Bank of America?

Bank of America is recognized globally as one of the world’s leading financial institutions.

Joining the organization provides access to:

  • Global career opportunities
  • Professional mentorship
  • Continuous learning
  • Leadership development
  • Diverse teams
  • International business exposure
  • Long-term career advancement

The organization is committed to helping young professionals build rewarding careers through structured development programs and meaningful work experiences.

Equal Employment Opportunity

Bank of America is committed to equal employment opportunity.

Employment decisions are made without discrimination based on factors including:

  • Race
  • Religion
  • Color
  • Sex
  • Sexual orientation
  • Gender identity
  • Gender expression
  • Age
  • National origin
  • Citizenship
  • Veteran status
  • Disability
  • Other legally protected characteristics

The company actively promotes diversity, inclusion, and equal access to employment opportunities.

Flexible Workplace Expectations

Depending on the role, employees may be expected to work from the office while benefiting from role-specific flexibility.

Specific attendance expectations are communicated during the hiring process and may vary by position.

Reasonable accommodations are available for eligible employees based on disability or sincerely held religious beliefs.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Visit the official Bank of America Careers website.
  2. Explore available internship and student opportunities.
  3. Review the eligibility requirements for their region.
  4. Select a suitable program or position.
  5. Submit an online application through the Bank of America Careers portal.

Applicants who applied previously may also be eligible to apply again depending on the specific recruitment cycle and program requirements.

Application Deadline

No application deadline has been specified.

Since recruitment timelines vary by program and location, interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible when suitable positions become available.

Official Website

Learn more and apply through the official Bank of America Careers portal:

 

Apply here 

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Internship opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Bank of America Campus Experience 2026: Launch Your Career with World-Class Internships, Training, Mentorship, and Global Networking Opportunities appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Three-Year UK Research Fellowship for Early-Career Humanities and Social Sciences Researchers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The British Academy has officially opened applications for its prestigious Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026—one of the United Kingdom’s leading funding opportunities for outstanding early-career researchers in the humanities and social sciences. Designed to help postdoctoral scholars establish independent research careers, the fellowship provides three years of funding to enable recipients to complete a significant piece of […]

The post British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Three-Year UK Research Fellowship for Early-Career Humanities and Social Sciences Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The British Academy has officially opened applications for its prestigious Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026—one of the United Kingdom’s leading funding opportunities for outstanding early-career researchers in the humanities and social sciences. Designed to help postdoctoral scholars establish independent research careers, the fellowship provides three years of funding to enable recipients to complete a significant piece of publishable research while strengthening their academic profile within a UK university or recognised research institution.

Funded by the UK Department for Science, Innovation & Technology (DSIT), the fellowship supports researchers in developing their research and teaching experience, expanding their professional networks, and improving their prospects of securing permanent academic positions.

If you are an early-career researcher looking to advance your academic career through high-quality independent research, the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 could be the ideal opportunity.

About the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships

The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships are awarded annually to an outstanding cohort of early-career researchers working in the humanities and social sciences.

The scheme is specifically designed to:

  • Support outstanding postdoctoral researchers as they establish independent academic careers.
  • Enable fellows to complete a substantial piece of publishable research.
  • Enhance research and teaching experience within a university environment.
  • Strengthen fellows’ academic profiles and career prospects.
  • Integrate fellows into communities of established scholars in their disciplines.

The programme provides recipients with the time, resources, mentorship, and institutional support needed to become future academic leaders.

Unlike short-term research grants, this fellowship offers sustained support over three years, allowing researchers to focus on producing high-quality research outputs while gaining valuable academic experience.

Purpose of the Fellowship

The British Academy created this fellowship to bridge the gap between completing a doctorate and securing a permanent academic position.

The programme helps early-career researchers by:

  • Developing independent research careers.
  • Supporting publication of significant academic work.
  • Encouraging excellence in humanities and social sciences research.
  • Building teaching and research experience.
  • Expanding academic networks.
  • Improving long-term employability within higher education.

The fellowship recognizes that the years immediately following a doctorate are often critical for developing an academic career.

Fellowship Duration

Successful applicants will receive funding for:

Three years

The fellowship is:

  • Non-renewable
  • Full-time
  • Hosted at an eligible UK institution

The earliest fellowship start date is:

1 September 2027

Funding Information

The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship operates under the Full Economic Costing (FEC) model.

Under this funding arrangement:

  • The British Academy funds 80% of eligible Full Economic Costs.
  • Funding includes salary costs.
  • Funding also covers directly allocated and indirect research costs under the FEC framework.

The fellowship is funded by the:

UK Department for Science, Innovation & Technology (DSIT).

What Fellows Will Do

Throughout the three-year fellowship, award holders will:

  • Conduct an independent research project.
  • Produce significant publishable research.
  • Build an academic publication record.
  • Gain valuable teaching experience.
  • Participate in the scholarly community of their host institution.
  • Collaborate with established academics.
  • Strengthen professional research networks.
  • Develop skills needed for long-term academic careers.

The primary emphasis of the fellowship is on completing research that contributes meaningfully to the humanities or social sciences.

Research Areas Supported

The fellowship supports research within:

Humanities

Examples include:

  • History
  • Philosophy
  • Literature
  • Languages
  • Archaeology
  • Classics
  • Religious Studies
  • Cultural Studies
  • Linguistics

Social Sciences

Examples include:

  • Sociology
  • Political Science
  • Economics
  • Human Geography
  • Anthropology
  • Education
  • Psychology (where eligible)
  • International Relations
  • Public Policy

Applicants should ensure that their proposed research falls within the British Academy’s funding remit.

Benefits of the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships

Successful fellows receive numerous professional and academic benefits.

These include:

Financial Support

  • Funding under the Full Economic Costing model.
  • 80% funding of eligible salary costs.
  • Support for eligible research-related costs.

Research Development

Fellows gain opportunities to:

  • Conduct independent research.
  • Produce high-impact publications.
  • Complete major research projects.
  • Develop long-term research agendas.

Academic Experience

Recipients benefit from:

  • Teaching opportunities.
  • Research collaboration.
  • Integration into academic departments.
  • Access to university research resources.

Career Advancement

The fellowship significantly strengthens applicants’ prospects for:

  • Permanent academic appointments.
  • Future research grants.
  • International collaborations.
  • Leadership roles in academia.

Professional Networking

Participants become part of:

  • Academic research communities.
  • Established scholarly networks.
  • Collaborative research environments.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must carefully review all eligibility requirements before applying.

To qualify, applicants must satisfy the following conditions.

1. Research Discipline

Applicants must be researchers in:

  • Humanities
  • Social Sciences

2. Host Institution

Applicants must be based at an eligible:

  • UK university
  • UK recognised research organisation

for the full duration of the fellowship.

3. Nationality Requirements

Applicants are eligible if they are:

  • British citizens
  • Nationals of any European Economic Area (EEA) country, regardless of where they obtained their doctorate

OR

Applicants of any nationality are eligible if they:

  • Hold a doctorate awarded by a UK university.

Applicants who do not fall into either category may still be eligible if they can demonstrate:

  • A strong prior association with the UK academic community.

4. Career Stage

Applicants must be early-career researchers.

Specifically, applicants are expected to have completed their:

Viva Voce examination between 1 April 2024 and 1 April 2027.

Applicants must also already hold postdoctoral status when the Research Awards Committee meets.

5. Permanent Academic Posts

Applicants are not eligible if they have ever held:

  • A permanent academic appointment
  • At any higher education institution
  • Either in the UK or overseas

This restriction also applies if the appointment occurred before the doctorate was awarded.

Certain exemptions may apply and require prior approval from the British Academy before submitting an application. Please check last round’s scheme guidance notes further information regarding exemption from this criterion.

Host Institution Requirements

The fellowship must be held at an eligible institution such as:

  • UK universities
  • UK recognised research organisations

The host institution will provide the academic environment in which fellows complete their research.

Referee Requirements

Applicants must nominate one referee.

The referee will complete a questionnaire through the Flexi-Grant® system.

Normally, the referee should be:

  • The external doctoral examiner

If unavailable:

  • Another suitably independent external academic.

The referee cannot be:

  • Proposed mentor
  • Doctoral supervisor
  • Internal examiner
  • Staff member from the proposed host institution

Application Process

Applications are submitted online using the British Academy’s:

Grant Management System (GMS) – Flexi-Grant®

Applicants are encouraged to prepare carefully before submitting.

Application Timeline

Applicants should note the following important dates.

Activity Date
Applications Open 1 July 2026
Application Deadline 30 September 2026 (17:00 BST)
Earliest Fellowship Start 1 September 2027
Fellowship Duration Three Years

Late submissions will not be accepted.

How to Apply

Applicants should:

  1. Review the official Scheme Guidance.
  2. Read the Application Guidance Notes carefully.
  3. Confirm eligibility.
  4. Secure an eligible UK host institution.
  5. Identify an eligible referee.
  6. Prepare the research proposal.
