  7. Submit the application through Flexi-Grant® before the deadline.
  8. Ensure institutional approval is completed before the submission deadline.

Scheme Guidance

Before applying, applicants are strongly encouraged to review:

  • Scheme Guidance Notes
  • Application Guidance Notes
  • Eligibility requirements
  • Funding rules
  • Assessment criteria

Carefully reviewing these documents can significantly improve the quality of an application.

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)

The British Academy provides a Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) section to help applicants understand the fellowship and application process.

Common topics covered include:

  • Eligibility requirements
  • Career stage requirements
  • Host institution eligibility
  • Referee requirements
  • Funding arrangements
  • Application process
  • Research proposal guidance
  • Fellowship conditions

Applicants are encouraged to review the FAQs before contacting the British Academy.

Applicant Support

Applicants who require assistance can access several support resources.

These include:

  • Scheme Guidance Notes
  • Application Guidance Notes
  • Frequently Asked Questions
  • Knowbot AI chatbot
  • British Academy Research Funding Team

Questions can also be directed via email to:

posts@thebritishacademy.ac.uk

Why Apply for the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship?

Securing a permanent academic position has become increasingly competitive.

The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowship provides a unique opportunity to strengthen an academic career by offering dedicated time, funding, and institutional support to conduct independent research.

Beyond financial support, fellows gain valuable experience in research, teaching, collaboration, and scholarly engagement—helping them build competitive academic portfolios and establish themselves within their disciplines.

For researchers seeking to publish influential work, develop new collaborations, and secure future academic opportunities, this fellowship represents one of the UK’s most prestigious early-career funding schemes.

Visit HERE to learn more about the British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships.

Conclusion

The British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 provide exceptional early-career researchers in the humanities and social sciences with the opportunity to establish themselves as independent scholars through a fully supported three-year research programme. Hosted at eligible UK universities and research organisations, the fellowship enables recipients to complete significant publishable research, gain valuable teaching and research experience, and integrate into leading academic communities.

With applications opening on 1 July 2026 and closing on 30 September 2026 at 17:00 BST, eligible researchers should begin preparing their proposals, confirming host institution arrangements, securing an appropriate referee, and reviewing the official guidance documents well before the deadline. For ambitious postdoctoral researchers aiming to strengthen their academic careers and contribute impactful research to the humanities and social sciences, this fellowship offers an outstanding pathway toward long-term academic success.

Discover more global Fellowship and Doctoral opportunities on OFY.

The post British Academy Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026–2027: Fully Funded Three-Year UK Research Fellowship for Early-Career Humanities and Social Sciences Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Free UN Online Course on Gender and Environment with Digital Certificate – Open for Global Learners
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The UN CC:Learn Open Online Course on Gender and Environment is currently accepting enrollments from learners around the world. This comprehensive self-paced course provides participants with an in-depth understanding of the relationship between gender equality and environmental sustainability, while equipping them with practical tools to integrate gender-responsive approaches into environmental policies, projects, and development initiatives. […]

The post Free UN Online Course on Gender and Environment with Digital Certificate – Open for Global Learners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UN CC:Learn Open Online Course on Gender and Environment is currently accepting enrollments from learners around the world. This comprehensive self-paced course provides participants with an in-depth understanding of the relationship between gender equality and environmental sustainability, while equipping them with practical tools to integrate gender-responsive approaches into environmental policies, projects, and development initiatives.

Developed through a collaboration between leading international organizations, including the Global Environment Facility (GEF), the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP), and UNITAR’s UN CC:Learn, the course serves as an important learning resource for individuals seeking to strengthen their knowledge of climate action, biodiversity conservation, sustainable development, and environmental governance.

Unlike traditional classroom programs, this online course allows learners to study at their own pace from anywhere in the world. Participants can access learning materials whenever convenient, making it suitable for both students and working professionals.

Why This Course Matters

Environmental challenges such as climate change, biodiversity loss, land degradation, pollution, and waste management do not affect everyone equally. Women, men, girls, and boys often experience environmental issues differently because of varying social, economic, and cultural roles.

Understanding these differences is becoming increasingly important for governments, NGOs, development agencies, researchers, and businesses seeking to design inclusive environmental policies and sustainable development strategies.

This course helps participants understand:

  • Why gender equality contributes to better environmental outcomes.
  • How environmental degradation affects different populations differently.
  • The importance of women’s leadership and empowerment in climate action.
  • How gender-responsive policies improve conservation and sustainable development.
  • International frameworks that support gender equality in environmental governance.

What You Will Learn

The course provides practical and theoretical knowledge covering several major environmental themes.

Participants will learn:

  1. The relationship between gender equality and environmental sustainability.
  2. Global environmental agreements that include gender considerations.
  3. How gender-responsive policies improve environmental outcomes.
  4. Practical approaches to integrating gender into environmental projects.
  5. Ways to promote women’s empowerment within biodiversity conservation.
  6. Gender considerations in climate change adaptation and mitigation.
  7. Gender issues relating to international waters.
  8. Sustainable land degradation management.
  9. Chemicals and waste management from a gender perspective.

Course Structure

The learning programme consists of six comprehensive modules, each designed to build practical knowledge step by step.

The modules include:

  • Introduction
  • Climate Change
  • International Waters
  • Biodiversity
  • Land Degradation
  • Chemicals and Waste

Each module takes approximately one hour to complete, resulting in a total learning time of around six hours. Since the course is self-paced, learners can complete it according to their own schedules without fixed deadlines.

Who Should Enroll?

The course has been designed for a wide variety of learners, including:

  • Students studying environmental sciences or sustainable development.
  • Climate change practitioners.
  • Biodiversity specialists.
  • Government officials working on environmental policies.
  • NGO staff involved in environmental conservation.
  • Development practitioners.
  • Researchers.
  • Community leaders.
  • Sustainability consultants.
  • Anyone interested in learning how gender equality contributes to environmental sustainability.

Certification

Participants who successfully complete all quizzes with a minimum score of 70% qualify for a UN Digital Certificate of Completion.

Important certification information:

  • A minimum score of 70% is required on every quiz.
  • Participants have three attempts for each quiz.
  • The course itself is free.
  • The official digital certificate becomes available upon payment of a USD 20 certification fee after successfully passing all quizzes.

Languages Available

The course is available in multiple languages, making it accessible to learners worldwide:

  • English
  • Spanish
  • French

International Partners

The course has been developed through collaboration among several globally recognized organizations, including:

  • Global Environment Facility (GEF)
  • United Nations Development Programme (UNDP)
  • UN CC:Learn
  • International Union for Conservation of Nature (IUCN)
  • UN Women
  • United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP)
  • Convention on Biological Diversity
  • United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)
  • United Nations Convention to Combat Desertification (UNCCD)
  • Basel, Rotterdam and Stockholm Conventions, among others.

Benefits of Taking the Course

Participants will benefit from:

  • Free access to high-quality UN learning materials.
  • Flexible self-paced online learning.
  • Practical knowledge applicable in environmental and development work.
  • Improved understanding of global gender and environmental frameworks.
  • Enhanced professional skills in sustainability and climate action.
  • Opportunity to earn a recognized UN Digital Certificate.
  • Better preparation for careers in environmental policy, NGOs, international organizations, research, and sustainable development.

Application Deadline

There is currently no application deadline.

Enrollment remains open, and interested learners may register and begin the course at any time.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can register online through the official UN CC:Learn course page:

Discover more global short courses opportunities on OFY here

The post Free UN Online Course on Gender and Environment with Digital Certificate – Open for Global Learners appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: German Bundestag International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) 2026 for U.S. Citizens: Fully Funded Five-Month Fellowship in Berlin with Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation and Travel Support
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
Young leaders passionate about democracy, governance, public policy, and international affairs now have an outstanding opportunity to gain first-hand experience inside one of Europe’s most influential democratic institutions. Applications are officially open for the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) Programme 2026, an initiative of the German Bundestag that enables outstanding young graduates to spend five months […]

The post German Bundestag International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) 2026 for U.S. Citizens: Fully Funded Five-Month Fellowship in Berlin with Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation and Travel Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Young leaders passionate about democracy, governance, public policy, and international affairs now have an outstanding opportunity to gain first-hand experience inside one of Europe’s most influential democratic institutions. Applications are officially open for the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) Programme 2026, an initiative of the German Bundestag that enables outstanding young graduates to spend five months in Berlin while learning about Germany’s parliamentary system and participating directly in legislative work.

The International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) is one of Germany’s flagship international exchange programmes designed to strengthen democratic values, encourage civic engagement, and foster long-term cooperation between Germany and partner countries. Established in 1986, the programme has provided thousands of young professionals from approximately 50 countries with practical parliamentary experience while building an international network of future leaders committed to democracy and public service.

For applicants in the United States, the programme is coordinated through the German Missions and offers selected participants the unique opportunity to work alongside a Member of the German Bundestag while also taking part in an extensive academic and professional development programme.

About the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS)

The International Parliamentary Scholarship is organized by the German Bundestag in cooperation with three of Germany’s leading universities:

  • Freie Universität Berlin
  • Humboldt-Universität zu Berlin
  • Technische Universität Berlin

Together, these institutions deliver a comprehensive programme that combines academic learning, professional training, seminars, networking opportunities, and practical parliamentary experience.

The fellowship is specifically designed for talented young graduates who have demonstrated a commitment to democratic principles and civic participation in their home countries. Through the programme, participants gain valuable insights into parliamentary decision-making, political institutions, governance structures, and international cooperation.

What Makes the IPS Programme Unique?

Unlike traditional academic scholarships, the IPS Programme offers participants the opportunity to experience politics from inside the German Parliament.

Participants spend five months in Berlin, where they become immersed in Germany’s democratic institutions and political culture.

One of the highlights of the programme is a three-month internship placement with a Member of the German Bundestag, allowing fellows to observe parliamentary work closely while contributing to daily legislative activities.

This practical experience is complemented by university courses, professional seminars, workshops, and political training sessions that deepen participants’ understanding of democratic governance.

The programme aims not only to educate participants but also to encourage international dialogue and strengthen democratic cooperation between Germany and participating countries.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows receive a comprehensive financial package that allows them to fully concentrate on learning and professional development throughout the programme.

Benefits include:

  • Monthly scholarship of 700 Euros
  • Free accommodation throughout the fellowship
  • Travel expenses
  • Health insurance coverage
  • Practical experience working in the German Parliament
  • Participation in seminars and professional training sessions
  • University courses delivered by leading Berlin universities
  • Networking opportunities with political leaders, scholars, and fellow participants from around the world
  • Exposure to Germany’s parliamentary decision-making processes
  • International leadership and intercultural experience

Programme Duration

The International Parliamentary Scholarship runs annually from:

March 1 to July 31

During these five months, participants complete academic, professional, and practical parliamentary activities designed to prepare them for future leadership roles in government, public administration, international organizations, academia, civil society, and policy development.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants from the United States must meet all of the following requirements:

  • Hold U.S. citizenship
  • Possess a completed college degree
  • Demonstrate very good German language proficiency (minimum B2 level)
  • Have a good understanding of German politics, society, and history
  • Be under the age of 30 at the beginning of the fellowship
  • Demonstrate a genuine interest in democratic governance and parliamentary systems
  • Show active engagement in promoting democratic values and civil society

The programme is particularly suited to graduates interested in careers in diplomacy, public administration, political science, international relations, law, public policy, journalism, governance, and civic leadership.

Selection Process

Applications undergo a competitive pre-selection process.

Shortlisted candidates are invited to participate in interviews conducted within the United States. Final scholarship recipients are selected by an independent panel appointed by the German Bundestag.

The rigorous selection process ensures that successful applicants possess strong academic credentials, leadership potential, commitment to democratic values, and the ability to serve as ambassadors for international cooperation.

Why You Should Apply

Participating in the International Parliamentary Scholarship offers far more than international study.

It provides an opportunity to:

  • Gain real-world parliamentary experience
  • Build international leadership skills
  • Develop professional networks across Germany and beyond
  • Learn directly from legislators and political experts
  • Enhance intercultural communication skills
  • Strengthen your understanding of democratic institutions
  • Improve your career prospects in government, international organizations, NGOs, academia, and public policy

The IPS Programme has become a globally respected fellowship because it combines academic excellence with practical political experience, creating future leaders who contribute to democratic development in their own countries.

Application Deadline

The deadline to submit applications for the International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) Programme 2026 is:

31 July 2025

Interested applicants should prepare their documents well before the deadline to ensure they have sufficient time to complete the application process.

How to Apply

Applicants should review the official programme information and submit their applications through the German Bundestag’s official IPS application portal.

Click here for Official Programme Information:

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here

 

The post German Bundestag International Parliamentary Scholarship (IPS) 2026 for U.S. Citizens: Fully Funded Five-Month Fellowship in Berlin with Monthly Stipend, Free Accommodation and Travel Support appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
